description:
You are acting as a configuration-manager whose job is to keep a simple audit trail for a data-pipeline.  
The pipeline already contains one configuration file:

        /home/user/data_pipeline/config.yaml   (0-byte file, deliberately left empty)

A directory for audit artefacts also exists:

        /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/

Goal
Create an audit log entry that records a fixed timestamp, the file name, and the SHA-256 digest of the configuration file.  
Only a single Linux command is needed to accomplish this.

Required final state
1. A **new file** called

        /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/config_audit.log

   must exist.

2. The file must contain exactly **one** line (ending with a single trailing newline).  
   The line must have the following pipe-separated format:

        2024-06-20T00:00:00Z|config.yaml|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

   • Column-1: the fixed timestamp 2024-06-20T00:00:00Z (do not substitute the current date).  
   • Column-2: the literal file name config.yaml (no path).  
   • Column-3: the SHA-256 digest of /home/user/data_pipeline/config.yaml.  
     Because the file is empty, the correct digest is precisely  
     e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

3. No other files may be changed or created.

Hints (do not reveal commands)
• You only need a single pipeline of standard Linux utilities to hash the file and prepend the fixed timestamp and file name before redirecting the result to the log.  
• Remember to place the log file inside /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/

The automated tests will:
• Verify that /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/config_audit.log exists.  
• Ensure the file contains exactly the single, correctly formatted line shown above followed by a newline.  
• Confirm that no other unexpected files have been created or modified.

Complete the task entirely from the terminal. You do not need root privileges.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before agent starts):
• Directory  /home/user/data_pipeline/           (permissions 755)
• File       /home/user/data_pipeline/config.yaml  – 0 bytes, mode 644
• Directory  /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/       (permissions 755)

Expected filesystem after successful completion:
• File /home/user/data_pipeline/logs/config_audit.log  (mode 644) containing exactly:
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2024-06-20T00:00:00Z|config.yaml|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\n
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
(no additional whitespace or lines)

SHA-256 digest that tests will recompute for /home/user/data_pipeline/config.yaml:
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
name: task_000000_6b63e4c3
